headline: Nasdaq Logs Longest Winning Streak Since 2021 as Investors Look Beyond War
summary: Oil prices have retreated.  The prospect of a peace deal that reopens the Strait of Hormuz has now powered the Nasdaq composite to its longest streak of daily gains since November 2021—a 10-session ascent that has lifted the tech-heavy index 14%.  The Dow Jones Industrial Average’s energy-hungry components have the blue-chip index lagging behind other major benchmarks, on Tuesday notching a 0.7% gain.

The Dow Jones Industrial Average’s energy-hungry components have the blue-chip index lagging behind other major benchmarks, on Tuesday notching a 0.7% gain.

headline: Morgan Stanley and BofA Expected to Post Higher Profits After Big Banks’ Mixed Results
summary: Investors are set to hear from Bank of America and Morgan Stanley on Wednesday after a string of big banks’ first-quarter earnings reports reflected solid U.S. consumer health.  Results from JPMorgan Chase, Citigroup, and Wells Fargo showed that measures of consumer credit, such as net charge-offs and delinquencies, were steady in the first quarter, even as borrowers faced higher energy prices prompted by the U.S.-Iran war.  Shares of Wells Fargo sunk 5.7% after the firm missed Wall Street's revenue and net interest income expectations, while JPMorgan's stock fell 0.8% and Citi's rose 2.6%.

Shares of Wells Fargo sunk 5.7% after the firm missed Wall Street's revenue and net interest income expectations, while JPMorgan's stock fell 0.8% and Citi's rose 2.6%.

headline: High Yield Bonds Are Riskier Than Previously Thought, Citi Says
summary: Citi  group is predicting a higher default rate for high-yield bonds, or debt issued by companies with so-called junk credit ratings.  Back in October, Citi recommended that it was a good time to “double down” on high-yield bonds, based on a healthy demand for risky assets like stocks alongside a rally in the U.S. debt market.  By the time February rolled around, actual defaults had reached 3.6%.

headline: Wall Street Traders Capitalize on Market Turbulence Tied to Iran War
summary: Middle East conflict and surging geopolitical tensions rattled markets in the first quarter.  JPMorgan Chase said Tuesday that its markets revenue jumped by 20% a year ago to $11.6 billion, a record, lifted by strong client activity across asset classes.  Volatile markets that prompt bank clients to reposition their portfolios and make big bets are typically boons for trading businesses.

Volatile markets that prompt bank clients to reposition their portfolios and make big bets are typically boons for trading businesses.

headline: Investment Banking Units Shine During Volatile Quarter
summary: Wall Street’s premier investment banking units put in solid performances during the first quarter, according to earnings reports from three of the nation’s largest banks on Tuesday.  JPMorgan Chase said that investment banking revenue rose 38% year over year to $3.1 billion for the quarter.  Citigroup said investment banking revenue increased 19% to $1.3 billion.

headline: Bank Earnings Show Consumer Resilience, Even as Pressures Mount
summary: When it comes to describing the U.S. economy, bank executives’ mot du jour is “resilient.”  “What we are seeing right now is that this is a resilient economy,” said Mike Santomassimo, Wells Fargo’s chief financial officer, on a call with reporters.  "The U.S. economy remained resilient in the quarter, with consumers still earning and spending and businesses still healthy,” JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon said in a statement.

headline: Big Banks Offer New Details on Exposure to Private Credit
summary: Banks sought to reassure investors on Tuesday by giving more detail on their lending to private credit funds in their earnings reports, and described the steps they have taken to limit their risk exposure.  Banks, though, have argued that this lending is highly structured and protected by collateral that the banks pick themselves.  Within that category, about 23% of the exposure was to business-development companies, or BDCs.

headline: U.S. Economy Remains Resilient in Face of Iran War, Banks Say
summary: American households and businesses are holding up despite a turbulent start to the year that has threatened to roil the U.S. economy.  JPMorgan Chase Citigroup  and  Wells Fargo  said customers continued to spend, borrow and invest in the first quarter, even as a war erupted in the Middle East and worries mounted about risks from artificial intelligence, inflation and private credit.  JPMorgan Chief Executive  Jamie Dimon  warned that while the broader U.S. economy was largely in good shape, a “complex set of risks” threatened to derail its trajectory.

JPMorgan Chief Executive  Jamie Dimon  warned that while the broader U.S. economy was largely in good shape, a “complex set of risks” threatened to derail its trajectory.

headline: Citi CEO Fraser Says Bank Is on Track to Hit Key Profitability Target in 2026
summary: Citigroup is on its way to hitting a target it set for a closely-watched profitability metric this year, Chief Executive Officer Jane Fraser said on Tuesday.  “We remain very much on track to deliver the 10-11% RoTCE target this year,” Fraser said, referring to return on tangible common equity, a commonly used measure of profitability among lenders.  Investors have monitored RoTCE and other measures, such as return on equity and efficiency ratios, to gauge Fraser’s progress in her mission to improve Citi’s performance firmwide.

Investors have monitored RoTCE and other measures, such as return on equity and efficiency ratios, to gauge Fraser’s progress in her mission to improve Citi’s performance firmwide.

headline: These Stocks Are Today’s Movers: Intel, American, Oracle, Novo Nordisk, Bloom Energy, Globalstar, JPMorgan, and More
summary: Stock futures were rising Tuesday as investors’ hopes for a peace deal between the U.S. and Iran strengthened.  Intel  climbed 1.4%, putting the chip maker on course to extend its winning streak to 10 sessions.  Shares have jumped 58% over the past nine trading days, rallying thanks to collaborations with  Tesla  and  Alphabet  Google, and Intel buying back full control of its chip factory in Ireland.

headline: How The Tate & Lyle (LSE:TATE) Investment Story Is Shifting After Fresh Analyst Revisions
summary: Tate & Lyle is back in focus after analysts trimmed fair value estimates from £5.01 to £4.84 per share and, in some cases, reset formal price targets such as the move from £5.40 to £5.00. Those shifts are being framed less as a reaction to a single event and more as a recalibration of how much investors might reasonably pay, with bullish and bearish voices drawing different conclusions from the same set of adjustments. Read on to see what is driving this evolving narrative and how you can...

summary: (Bloomberg) -- Australian consumer sentiment tumbled the most since 2020 while business confidence also nosedived as soaring fuel prices coupled with higher interest rates triggered dramatic falls in both readings.Consumer sentiment plummeted 12.5% to 80.1 points in April, Westpac Banking Corp. said in a statement on Tuesday.
